ground_truth.title: Small Business Enterprise Certification Programs
ground_truth.main_content: Miami-Dade County supports the growth and success of small businesses through a range of certification programs that promote participation in County contracting opportunities. Certified firms may qualify under one or more of the following categories: Small Business Enterprise – Architectural & Engineering (SBE-A&E), Small Business Enterprise – Construction (SBE-CON), Small Business Enterprise – Goods and Services (SBE-GS), Local Developing Business (LDB), Disadvantaged Business Enterprise (DBE), and Airport Concessionaire Disadvantaged Business Enterprise (ACDBE). Certification for a SBE firm is valid for a three-year period. To validate continuing eligibility, SBD may conduct a random audit within the three-year certification period. Additional information may be obtained at the Small Business Enterprise Programs or call the Small Business Development office at 305-375-3111. Apply for SBE Certification Programs online through the Business Management Workforce System. For questions or assistance, contact the SPD Certification Unit at 305-375-3111 or spd@miamidade.gov. The SBE-GS program is designed for firms providing goods or services other than construction, architectural, or engineering services. Eligibility requires an active Miami-Dade County Local Business Tax receipt for at least one year, a physical location in Miami-Dade County, personal net worth not exceeding $3.5 million, and average gross receipts over the past 3 years not exceeding $8 million. The SBE-A&E program is for independent architecture, engineering, mapping, or landscape architecture firms with similar eligibility requirements but with a $6.5 million average gross receipts limit for most services. The SBE-CON program is for independent construction firms with average gross receipts limits ranging from $8 million to $15 million depending on the specialty trade classification. The LDB program is designed for firms providing aeronautical services at Miami International Airport. The DBE program is a federal program that ensures equal opportunity in transportation contracting markets and addresses the effects of discrimination in transportation contracting. The ACDBE program increases opportunities for minority and women-owned small businesses to operate as concessionaires in airports.
with: Miami-Dade County's Small Business Enterprise (SBE) and Local Developing Business (LDB) Certification programs were created for any business entity providing construction, architectural, engineering, goods, services, and aeronautical support services.
Certification for a SBE firm is valid for a three-year period.
Personal net worth for each owner must not exceed $3.5 million.
Must have a physical location in Miami-Dade County (no virtual offices).
Apply for SBE Certification Programs online through the Business Management Workforce System.
